summary: Created launch_all.py as a standalone supervisor that starts the mlb_model shadow engine and sports_bot_v2 bot concurrently, logs both to files, polls each process, and restarts an exited child after a delay.
Added explicit BRIDGE ALL STALE logging to model_bridge.py when no actionable recommendations remain after NO_TRADE filtering and latest-per-slug dedup.
Kept scope strictly limited to launcher + one bridge log addition; no bot core, dashboard, DB, or model code changes.
tests_run: Confirmed launcher stdout showed both child processes starting: shadow_engine and bot_core.
Read logs/shadow_engine.log and confirmed shadow engine startup plus live registry/model startup output.
Read logs/bot_core_launcher.log and confirmed bot startup output under launcher supervision.
Observed launcher process itself start correctly, but I did not manually kill a child process in this run, so watchdog restart behavior was not directly exercised end-to-end.
watchdog_tested: partial — launcher started both processes successfully, but no manual child kill/restart cycle was induced during this verification window
bridge_all_stale_observed: no — shadow engine produced startup/live feed output immediately, so the explicit all-stale log did not appear in the short observed window
gate_reject_ages: not observed in launcher-run bot log during this short window; prior direct bridge run showed rec_age rejects, but this launcher verification window primarily captured fresh process startup
result: Launcher started both child processes successfully.
shadow_engine.log showed live-game/model startup activity, confirming stdout/stderr redirection to file works.
bot_core_launcher.log showed bot startup activity, confirming concurrent launch works.
No crashes were introduced by the launcher or the BRIDGE ALL STALE addition.
risks: I did not manually kill a subprocess, so restart logging (LAUNCHER: exited/restarted) was not directly observed in this run.
The host terminated the launcher session after the observation window, so longer-loop bridge behavior under concurrent launch was not fully observed here.
Existing non-fatal DB duplicate-column startup log remains in bot logs and may be worth cleanup in a later task.
next_recommended_task: LAUNCH_002 — run a longer supervised session, manually kill one child to verify watchdog restart logs, and capture BRIDGE ALL STALE / BRIDGE GATE REJECT/PASS behavior over multiple loops.
